image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: One delivers instructions; the other delivers a piece of the pathogen.
Context visual: mRNA vaccines teach our cells how to fight an infection by delivering genetic instructions.
Escalation visual: Traditional vaccines, however, often use weakened or inactivated parts of the virus.
Evidence visual: Studies suggest mRNA could be faster to develop than traditional methods.
Pattern Interrupt visual: But both types can effectively train our immune systems.
Payoff visual: Ultimately, each platform has its advantages. Understanding them helps us make informed choices.
Cta visual: Curious about vaccines? Like and follow for more science insights!
